title: Sort Colors (Dutch Flag)
code: case 0: (nums[low], nums[mid]) = (nums[mid], nums[low]); low++; mid++; break;

frames.caption: Initialize three pointers: low=0 (0s boundary), mid=0 (scanner), high=5 (2s boundary). Partition [2,0,2,1,1,0] into 0s | 1s | 2s in one pass.
frames.note: Read the value under `mid`. Three regions are invariant: a[0..low-1] are all 0s, a[high+1..] are all 2s, and a[low..mid-1] are all 1s. `mid` is the scanner that grows those regions one element at a time, which is what makes this a single O(n) pass with O(1) extra space.

frames.caption: Read nums[mid] = nums[0] = 2.
frames.note: Read the value under `mid`. Three regions are invariant: a[0..low-1] are all 0s, a[high+1..] are all 2s, and a[low..mid-1] are all 1s. `mid` is the scanner that grows those regions one element at a time, which is what makes this a single O(n) pass with O(1) extra space.

frames.caption: val 2: swap mid↔high(5), then high→4. mid STAYS at 0 — the swapped-in value is unexamined.
frames.note: Value is 2: swap it up to the `high` boundary so it joins the settled 2s, then shrink `high`. Critically, do NOT advance `mid` — the element just swapped IN from `high` has never been examined, so we must re-read this same index on the next iteration.

frames.caption: Read nums[mid] = nums[0] = 0.
frames.note: Read the value under `mid`. Three regions are invariant: a[0..low-1] are all 0s, a[high+1..] are all 2s, and a[low..mid-1] are all 1s. `mid` is the scanner that grows those regions one element at a time, which is what makes this a single O(n) pass with O(1) extra space.

frames.caption: val 0: swap mid↔low(0), then low→1, mid→1. The 0 joins the settled 0s.
frames.note: Value is 0: swap it down to the `low` boundary so it joins the settled 0s, then advance BOTH `low` and `mid`. We can advance `mid` here because whatever came from `low` was already scanned (it was a 1, or low==mid so it is this very cell) — never an unexamined value.

frames.caption: Read nums[mid] = nums[1] = 0.
frames.note: Read the value under `mid`. Three regions are invariant: a[0..low-1] are all 0s, a[high+1..] are all 2s, and a[low..mid-1] are all 1s. `mid` is the scanner that grows those regions one element at a time, which is what makes this a single O(n) pass with O(1) extra space.

frames.caption: val 0: swap mid↔low(1), then low→2, mid→2. The 0 joins the settled 0s.
frames.note: Value is 0: swap it down to the `low` boundary so it joins the settled 0s, then advance BOTH `low` and `mid`. We can advance `mid` here because whatever came from `low` was already scanned (it was a 1, or low==mid so it is this very cell) — never an unexamined value.

frames.caption: Read nums[mid] = nums[2] = 2.
frames.note: Read the value under `mid`. Three regions are invariant: a[0..low-1] are all 0s, a[high+1..] are all 2s, and a[low..mid-1] are all 1s. `mid` is the scanner that grows those regions one element at a time, which is what makes this a single O(n) pass with O(1) extra space.

frames.caption: val 2: swap mid↔high(4), then high→3. mid STAYS at 2 — the swapped-in value is unexamined.
frames.note: Value is 2: swap it up to the `high` boundary so it joins the settled 2s, then shrink `high`. Critically, do NOT advance `mid` — the element just swapped IN from `high` has never been examined, so we must re-read this same index on the next iteration.

frames.caption: Read nums[mid] = nums[2] = 1.
frames.note: Read the value under `mid`. Three regions are invariant: a[0..low-1] are all 0s, a[high+1..] are all 2s, and a[low..mid-1] are all 1s. `mid` is the scanner that grows those regions one element at a time, which is what makes this a single O(n) pass with O(1) extra space.

frames.caption: val 1: already in place, just mid→3.
frames.note: 1 is already in its final region — it belongs in the middle band — so we leave it and just advance `mid`. No swap needed; this is the cheap case that keeps the constant factor tiny.

frames.caption: Read nums[mid] = nums[3] = 1.
frames.note: Read the value under `mid`. Three regions are invariant: a[0..low-1] are all 0s, a[high+1..] are all 2s, and a[low..mid-1] are all 1s. `mid` is the scanner that grows those regions one element at a time, which is what makes this a single O(n) pass with O(1) extra space.

frames.caption: val 1: already in place, just mid→4.
frames.note: 1 is already in its final region — it belongs in the middle band — so we leave it and just advance `mid`. No swap needed; this is the cheap case that keeps the constant factor tiny.

frames.caption: mid (4) > high (3): stop. Array is partitioned into 0s | 1s | 2s — sorted.
frames.note: Once `mid` passes `high` every cell has been classified into its region: 0s, then 1s, then 2s. One pass, O(n) time, O(1) space, in place — and stable region boundaries mean no element is touched more than a constant number of times.
